summary: For grooming, start with the lowest-stress coat and hygiene tasks.
guardrail: Avoid piling on too many grooming tasks in one session.

summary: For sitting, match the task to the length of the visit.
guardrail: Choose only one sitting task at a time.

summary: For training, start with a simple obedience goal.
For walking, begin with the next practical walk window.
guardrail: Do not overbook back-to-back walk tasks.

summary: For the Dog Cafe section, meal choices are the main action.
guardrail: Keep cafe bookings to one meal slot.

summary: For veterinary visits, choose the medical task that matches the concern.
guardrail: Always use the owner-provided reason and avoid inventing treatment details.
